description: Required Workout Time & Getting Started Card Update - Enforce workout time in schedules and rewrite onboarding card with clear two-step flow

userStories.title: Make time field required in shared WorkoutSchedule type
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the WorkoutSchedule.time field to be required so that all downstream code enforces a time value.
acceptanceCriteria: Change `time?: string` to `time: string` in WorkoutSchedule interface in packages/shared/src/types.ts
Fix any TypeScript errors caused by this change in services/api and apps/web (add time values where WorkoutSchedule objects are constructed without one)
Rebuild shared package with `cd packages/shared && yarn build`
Typecheck passes (`cd services/api && npx tsc --noEmit` and `cd apps/web && npx tsc --noEmit`)

userStories.title: Validate required time in API schedule endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the API to reject schedule saves that omit a time value, returning a 400 validation error.
acceptanceCriteria: PUT /burn-buddies/:id/schedule returns 400 with message 'Workout time is required' if time is missing or empty string
POST /burn-squads returns 400 with message 'Workout time is required' if workoutSchedule is provided without time
PUT /burn-squads/:id/settings returns 400 with message 'Workout time is required' if workoutSchedule is provided without time
Existing API tests in services/api/src/routes/__tests__/ updated to include time in all schedule payloads
New test cases added that verify 400 is returned when time is missing from schedule
Tests pass (`cd services/api && yarn test`)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Require time in web Burn Buddy schedule editor
userStories.description: As a user editing my Burn Buddy workout schedule on the web, I must provide a time before I can save so my calendar events have a specific time.
acceptanceCriteria: Time input (<input type='time'>) is always visible when editing a schedule in apps/web/src/app/(main)/burn-buddies/[id]/page.tsx (not hidden until days are selected)
Save Schedule button is disabled until both at least one day AND a time are provided
Inline validation message 'Please select a workout time' appears below the time input if user attempts to save without a time
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Require time in web Burn Squad creation and settings forms
userStories.description: As a user creating or editing a Burn Squad on the web, I must provide a time when setting a workout schedule.
acceptanceCriteria: Time input is always visible in squad creation form (apps/web/src/app/(main)/burn-squads/new/page.tsx) when at least one day is selected
Squad creation form prevents submission if days are selected but time is empty, with inline validation message
Squad settings editing prevents saving schedule without a time, with inline validation message
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Require time in mobile Burn Buddy and Burn Squad schedule forms
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I must provide a workout time when setting a schedule for a Burn Buddy or Burn Squad.
acceptanceCriteria: Time input is required in mobile Burn Buddy schedule editor — save is disabled without a time
Time input is required in mobile Burn Squad creation/settings forms — save is disabled without a time
Visible inline error message appears when user tries to save without a time
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update Getting Started card on web
userStories.description: As a new web user, I see a Getting Started card that clearly tells me to first add a friend, then request to be a Burn Buddy.
acceptanceCriteria: Card text in apps/web/src/components/GettingStartedCard.tsx is rewritten with two numbered steps: 1) Add a friend (search by email on the Friends page), 2) Request to be a Burn Buddy with that friend
No mention of Burn Squads anywhere in the card
Dismiss button (×) and re-enable toggle on Account page still work unchanged
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Update Getting Started card on mobile
userStories.description: As a new mobile user, I see a Getting Started card that matches the web's two-step Burn Buddy onboarding flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Card text in apps/mobile/src/components/GettingStartedCard.tsx displays the same two-step flow: 1) Add a friend by email, 2) Request to be a Burn Buddy
No mention of Burn Squads in the card
Fix branding from 'buddyburn' to 'BurnBuddy'
Typecheck passes
